bullet_points: Load the dataset and parse chemical formulas to extract metal-to-oxygen ratios.
Identify stoichiometric pairs of oxide precursors and carbonate products, retaining all distinct polymorph pairs and flagging them with a polymorph count.
Filter pairs to ensure both oxide and carbonate are within the 0.1 eV/atom energy above hull threshold.
Produce a summary visualization (bar chart) of matched pairs per capture element and print key statistics (total pairs, pairs per element, energy-above-hull distributions).
Save the resulting matched pairs dataset.
Calculate reaction enthalpy (ΔH) at 0 K using the reference energy for CO₂ (-11.17 eV/formula unit).
Estimate Debye temperature (Θ_D) using the Lindemann criterion (molar mass from formula, volume per atom, and density).
Calculate Gibbs free energy (ΔG) at 700 K and 0.15 bar CO₂ partial pressure, including vibrational entropy and fixed CO₂ gas-phase entropy (0.00269 eV/K).
Print mean, median, and range of ΔH and ΔG per capture element family.
Generate a multi-panel box plot showing distributions of ΔH and ΔG per capture element.
Save the calculated thermodynamic properties.
Calculate molar volume change (ΔV) for each reaction pair.
Apply a filter to exclude candidates with |ΔV| > 20%.
Calculate the cohesive energy proxy for the remaining candidates.
Print a summary table of candidates surviving the ΔV filter per capture element.
Generate a scatter plot of ΔV (%) vs. ΔG for all pairs, marking the 20% threshold.
Save the filtered dataset.
Train a Random Forest classifier to predict `is_stable` status; print cross-validated ROC-AUC, precision, recall, and F1 score.
Generate a feature importance bar chart for the classifier.
Perform sensitivity analysis on the top 10 "Goldilocks" candidates by varying CO₂ partial pressure (0.05 to 0.5 bar).
Generate a line plot of ΔG vs. CO₂ partial pressure for these candidates.
Save model metrics and sensitivity results.
Construct a 2D Pareto front plot of ΔG (at 700 K) vs. cohesive energy proxy, annotating the top 10 Pareto-optimal candidates.
Rank candidates based on proximity to the "optimal-delta" zone.
Print a ranked table of the top 20 candidates (formula, ID, element, ΔG, ΔV, cohesive energy, stability, crystal system).
Save the Pareto plot and the final ranked list.

Write the final Results section of the report, structured by analysis step (Reaction Mapping, Thermodynamic Landscape, Mechanical Filtering, Stability Prediction, Sensitivity Analysis, Pareto Ranking).
Explicitly reference all quantitative results (e.g., number of pairs, classifier AUC, top ΔG values) and plots generated in previous steps.
Discuss elemental family trends, the impact of mechanical constraints, and implications for industrial CO₂ capture.
Ensure the report is self-contained and suitable for direct inclusion in a scientific paper.
